questions: Should the preview toggle (switch between shots panel and Remotion preview) be a persistent preference or reset each time the editor opens?
For the condensed (compact) layout: should we add a third 'Shots' tab alongside Preview and Properties, or replace Preview entirely when not on the editor page?
When a shot has both a final video and images, should the thumbnail prefer the video thumbnail or the first image?
Should clicking a shot in the panel do anything (e.g., navigate to shot editor, select it), or is it purely a drag source?

success_criteria.criterion: On pages other than /tools/video-editor, the top pane shows the ShotsPanel instead of PreviewPanel
On /tools/video-editor, the PreviewPanel (Remotion preview) is shown as before — no regression
Shots are rendered in a compact horizontal-scrolling grid showing thumbnails and names
Shots are draggable from the panel onto the timeline using existing setShotDragData format
Dropping a shot on the timeline creates clips correctly (video if available, images otherwise) via existing useExternalDrop
When clips are selected on the timeline, corresponding shots are highlighted in the panel
Search input filters shots by name
'Related only' filter toggle shows only shots matching the current clip selection
Remotion preview playback continues to function (time sync, play/pause) even when ShotsPanel is displayed
User can toggle back to Remotion preview from the shots panel
No new dependencies added — reuses existing DnD utilities and shot hooks
Panel handles empty state (no shots) and loading state gracefully
Condensed layout includes Shots tab or replaces Preview when not on editor page
Grid shows 4-5 shots per visible row with tight spacing

assumptions: The 'main video travel tool page' where we keep the preview is specifically /tools/video-editor (the standalone editor page), not /tools/travel-between-images
The Remotion preview portal can be mounted into a hidden div to keep playback/time sync working while showing the shots panel
We don't need dnd-kit for the ShotsPanel since we only need drag-out (no reordering) — native HTML5 drag with setShotDragData is sufficient
ShotsContext (useShots) is already available in the component tree where VideoEditorShell renders, since it's provided at a higher level
The shot card in ShotsPanel will be a new minimal component rather than reusing SortableShotItem, which carries too much drop/reorder machinery
Clicking a shot in the panel is not required — it's purely a drag source and visual indicator of selection state
